text.title: Objection Bank Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Objection Bank. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every objection entry has ≥1 evidence link (call timestamp, reply URL, replay ID). No evidence = speculation, not data.
rule.rationale: Without evidence the bank becomes folklore + cannot be audited.

rule.statement: Entries ranked by frequency_count over last 30 days. Stale entries drop down naturally.
rule.rationale: Unranked banks default to whatever's loudest, not most expensive.

rule.statement: Each entry has a response template (≤150 words) verified against actual replies that converted.
rule.rationale: Untested templates compound the objection rather than dispelling.

rule.statement: Top-10 objections flow into FAQ + landing copy within 14 days of becoming top-10.
rule.rationale: Objections that never reach FAQ keep killing deals.

rule.statement: Named owner runs weekly review: new objections logged, stale ones decay, top-10 audit.
rule.rationale: Without weekly review the bank rots in 60 days.

rule.statement: Strip personal info (names, companies) from objection records before sharing; keep only the objection pattern.
rule.rationale: Quoting customers by name destroys trust.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-objection-bank.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
